senses.definition: a situation or activity that is not productive

examples.x: The investment turned out to be a dead loss, with no return on the money spent.
The project was a dead loss from the beginning, with no clear goals or direction.

senses.definition: to have an undesirable, unfortunate, or disappointing outcome

examples.x: He has come to grief in his career because of his bad attitude.
If they don't prepare well for the competition, they will come to grief.
If you don't start studying seriously, you will come to grief in your exams.
The project came to grief when the team missed a crucial deadline.
Their business is coming to grief because of the pandemic.

senses.definition: with no chance of succeeding in a competition

examples.x: He has been out of the running for the promotion since he missed the deadline.
She has been out of the running for the job since she failed the interview.
They are out of the running for the championship this year, as they have lost all their matches so far.
They were out of the running for the award after they received negative reviews from the judges.

senses.definition: far from the correct or expected result

examples.x: His estimate was wide off the mark, and the project ended up costing much more than he predicted.
His prediction was wide of the mark, as the election results turned out to be completely different.
She has been consistently wide off the mark in her sales forecasts.
Their analysis is currently wide of the mark, as they haven't taken all the necessary data into account.
The prediction about the stock market was wide off the mark.

senses.definition: a situation or series of events that is marked by a sequence of humorous mistakes, mishaps, or misunderstandings

examples.x: The meeting has turned into a comedy of errors, with everyone talking over each other and misunderstanding the agenda.
The play is a comedy of errors, filled with mistaken identities and miscommunications.
The project was a comedy of errors from the beginning, with missed deadlines and incorrect information.

senses.definition: a period of hardship, struggle, or adversity

examples.x: If they don't prepare well, they will face the dog days of a difficult exam.
She has been through the dog days of a divorce, with legal battles and emotional turmoil.
The company is going through the dog days of a financial crisis, with layoffs and budget cuts.
We're in the dog days of the pandemic, struggling with the new variant and rising cases.

senses.definition: to lose the chance to take advantage of something, particularly a good opportunity

examples.x: He has missed the boat for the job offer, as he didn't respond to the email in time.
If you don't submit your application soon, you'll miss the boat for the scholarship.
She missed the bus for the concert tickets, as they sold out within minutes.
They're working hard to meet the deadline, as they don't want to miss the bus for the new project.

senses.definition: someone who is prone to lose something that is of great value or importance to them, such as their position, job, title, etc.

examples.x: He was accused of sexual harassment, and after the investigation, he became a dead man walking at the university.
He's been warned about his poor performance, and now he's a dead man walking at the company.
The scandal has been leaked to the press, and now the politician is a dead man walking.

senses.definition: someone or something that has never succeeded or is very unlikely to succeed in the future

examples.x: The actor's last few movies have bombed at the box office, and now he's a dead duck in Hollywood.
The company's outdated technology has made it a dead duck in the market.
The project is behind schedule and over budget, and it's becoming a dead duck.

senses.definition: to suddenly fail at something

examples.x: He has come a cropper in his business ventures, losing money in every investment.
If she doesn't study harder, she'll come a cropper in her exams.
The project is running behind schedule, and it looks like they're going to come a cropper.

senses.definition: a situation that shows no signs of progress or improvement

examples.x: He has tried every avenue to find a job in his field, but he's come to a dead end.
The company's outdated business model has led it to a dead end in the market.
The negotiations have reached a dead end, with no progress made on either side.

senses.definition: used to describe a situation or plan that has come to a complete halt or has become unproductive, with little to no chance of progress or success

examples.x: His career has been dead in the water since he lost his job a year ago.
The negotiations are dead in the water as both sides refuse to compromise.
The project is dead in the water without proper funding and support.

senses.definition: to be no longer effective or of much use

examples.x: His ideas about technology have passed their sell-by date, and now he's struggling to keep up with the industry.
She has passed her sell-by date as a pop star, and her music is no longer popular.
The company's products are becoming irrelevant, and they're in danger of passing their sell-by date in the market.

senses.definition: having failed in doing or achieving something and unable to continue

examples.x: He has been fighting the illness for weeks, and now he's down for the count.
The company is struggling to stay afloat, and it looks like they might be down for the count.
The team is down for the count after losing their star player to an injury.

senses.definition: to experience a fall in social or financial status

examples.x: She has come down in the world since her divorce, losing her social status and many of her friends.
The family's financial troubles have caused them to come down in the world, and they're selling their assets to survive.

senses.definition: someone who was once significant or important but is now considered outdated or no longer influential

examples.x: Her fashion sense is seen as yesterday's woman, and now she's struggling to keep up with the latest trends.
His management style is seen as yesterday's man, and now the company is struggling to keep up with the competition.
The marketing campaign is becoming yesterday's woman in the market, and it's failing to attract new customers.
The musician was yesterday's man after his music went out of style.
The product line is becoming yesterday's man in the market, and sales are declining rapidly.

senses.definition: to be unable to come up with ideas or solutions to a problem

examples.x: I've been searching for my keys all morning, but I keep drawing a blank.
If they don't have any leads, they'll likely draw a blank in the investigation.
The team searched for hours, but they drew a blank in finding the missing item.

senses.definition: used to say that there are clear indications or signals that something negative or disastrous is going to happen soon, and that it is likely to be unavoidable
to defeat a person or a group thoroughly, often in a very humiliating manner

examples.x: The chess master outplayed his opponent, taking him to the cleaners with clever moves.

senses.definition: an imaginary or symbolic prize given to the person who finishes in last place or performs poorly in a competition or event

examples.x: Our college team took the wooden spoon in the inter-collegiate league this season.
They finished with the wooden spoon after losing a penalty shoot-out.

senses.definition: to overwhelm or defeat someone completely and unexpectedly
